title: KCNA reports Kim Jong Un guided test-firing of new weapons at munitions factory inspection
summary: North Korean state media KCNA reported Kim Jong Un visited a weapons factory, reviewed precision guided weapons production, and guided test-firing activities. The Supreme Leader ordered production capacity expansion by approximately 2.5 times.
contents:
## KCNA Report: Kim Jong Un Munitions Factory Inspection

### State Media Announcement
The Korean Central News Agency (KCNA) reported that Supreme Leader Kim Jong Un visited a weapons factory to review the production status of multipurpose precision guided weapons. The visit included direct observation of production processes and guidance of test-firing activities.

### Production Directives
Kim Jong Un issued orders to expand the current production capacity by approximately 2.5 times, emphasizing the importance of strengthening war deterrent capabilities. Military officials and munitions industry leaders accompanied the inspection tour.

### Weapons Systems Inspected
State media reporting indicated focus on:
- Multipurpose precision guided weapons
- Tactical guided weapons production lines
- Quality control and testing procedures

### Pattern of Activity
This factory visit continues a series of weapons-related inspections in recent weeks. Kim has visited multiple factories, a nuclear-powered submarine under construction, and oversaw missile test launches — all ahead of the anticipated Workers' Party Ninth Congress.

### Propaganda Context
State media coverage of the inspection follows established patterns of demonstrating leadership attention to military capabilities. The reporting serves domestic legitimacy purposes while signaling deterrent capability development to external audiences.

### Assessment
While KCNA reliably confirms that leadership visits occurred, specific production claims and capability assessments require independent verification through satellite imagery and other intelligence sources.

**Source**: KCNA, Pyongyang Today monitoring, state media aggregation
